response.justification: Bogdan is a capable and broadly experienced software engineer with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, clean code), but he is fundamentally misaligned with the core technical requirements of this role. His primary expertise is TypeScript and Node.js; Java appears only marginally in two roles and Kotlin is absent entirely. There is no Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience, no evidence of JVM internals knowledge, and no familiarity with enterprise Java build tooling (Maven/Gradle) or application servers. His one IDE-adjacent role at National Instruments involved a C-based IDE, not JVM tooling, and lasted only six months. The gap between his actual skill set and the role's requirements is too wide to bridge at the mid-senior level.
strengths: Consistent application of software craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, pairing) across multiple senior roles
Prior experience contributing to an IDE product at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI), demonstrating exposure to developer tooling concerns
Proven ability to work on high-scale, low-latency systems (millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby), indicating systems-level thinking
concerns: No Java or Kotlin as a primary language; Java is mentioned only peripherally in two roles with no depth demonstrated
Completely absent Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience
No evidence of JVM internals, build tool knowledge (Maven/Gradle), or application server integration experience
